channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Universities warn international students to come back to campus before Trump inauguration
item.description: Schools are also warning that international students should travel with additional documentation of their connection to their American college.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Oregon sheriff concerned about letters asking people to track possible immigrants
item.description: LINCOLN CITY, Ore. (AP) — A sheriff in Oregon says he has contacted the FBI and is increasingly concerned about a letter circulating in his rural, coastal community asking people to write down the license plate numbers of possible immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Government funding difficulties create gloom for federal workers before Christmas
item.description: Johnny Zuagar says he’s tried to hide his worries about a potential government shutdown from his three boys as he weighs how much to spend on Christmas presents.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NORAD's Santa tracker was a Cold War morale boost. Now it attracts millions of kids
item.description: The Christmas tradition has become nearly global in scope: Children from around the world track Santa Claus as he sweeps across the earth, delivering presents and defying time. Each year, at least 100,000 kids call into the North American Aerospace Defense Command to inquire about Santa’s location.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk helped kill a congressional spending bill. But much of what he spread was misinformation
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's billionaire ally Elon Musk played a key role this week in killing a bipartisan funding proposal that would have prevented a government shutdown, railing against the plan in a torrent of more than 100 X posts that included multiple false claims.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: House approves funding bill and sends to Senate hours before government shutdown deadline
item.description: Hours to go before a midnight government shutdown, the House has approved a new plan from House Speaker Mike Johnson.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Farnoush Amiri And Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ohio lawmakers approve charging up to $750 for police and jail videos
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — People seeking copies of police and jail videos in Ohio may have to pay up to $75 for each hour if Gov. Mike DeWine signs a measure approved by the state legislature this week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin school shooting by first-semester student lasted 8 minutes: official
item.description: The 15-year-old girl who police say killed a teacher and teen student in a shooting at a Christian school Wisconsin was in her first semester at the school and the attack lasted eight minutes, school officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: International students urged to return to US campuses before Trump inauguration
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A growing number of U.S. colleges and universities are advising international students to return to campus before President-elect Donald Trump is inaugurated, over concerns that he might impose travel bans like he did during his first administration.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Young nuns: Two diverse paths to a rare life of faith
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Less than 1% of Catholic nuns in the United States today are 30 or younger. Seyram Adzokpa and Zoey Stapleton are two of the young women who have made the rare decision to join a religious community and begin the long process to become nuns. Here are their stories.
item.dc:creator: JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More student loans canceled in final round of forgiveness before Biden leaves office
item.description: With the latest round of relief, Biden has now canceled an unprecedented $180 billion in federal student loans through existing programs, covering 4.9 million Americans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republicans scramble for funding plan C as shutdown deadline draws near
item.description: Congress is racing toward a shutdown at the end of the day Friday, and Republicans appear no closer to finding a path forward that will keep the lights on and appease President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: FDA updates definition of 'healthy' on food labels for first time in 30 years
item.description: A new rule from the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) will update what it means for food to be labeled “healthy” for the first time in 30 years, a move that aligns with current nutrition science and is meant to help consumers better navigate food labels.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Man accused in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing faces federal charge that's eligible for death penalty
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO was whisked back to New York by plane and helicopter Thursday to face new federal charges of stalking and murder, which could bring the death penalty if he's convicted.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, LARRY NEUMEISTER and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 1 dead in small plane crash near home of NFL player Dion Dawkins
item.description: One person died in a small plane crash Thursday morning close to the house of Buffalo Bills offensive lineman Dion Dawkins, police said.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, Fox 5

item.title: California political operative allegedly acted as illegal agent of China: DOJ
item.description: A California man who helped an individual get elected to a local California city council is accused of acting as an illegal agent for the People's Republic of China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House rejects Johnson's plan B to prevent shutdown
item.description: The House on Thursday torpedoed Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan B to avert a government shutdown and suspend the debt limit, dealing a blow to the GOP leader and catapulting the conference back to square one as it stares down Friday's funding deadline.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Snow, ice and strong winds make for dangerous conditions in upper Midwest
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — A fast-moving winter storm brought snow, ice, strong winds and bitter cold to much of the upper Midwest, snarling traffic in the Twin Cities and forcing North Dakota officials to shut down an interstate highway.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and JIM SALTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Immigration drives US population growth to highest rate in 23 years as residents pass 340 million
item.description: Immigration in 2024 drove U.S. population growth to its fastest rate in 23 years as the nation surpassed 340 million residents, the U.S. Census Bureau said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Appeals court removes prosecutor Fani Willis from Georgia election case against Trump and others
item.description: A state appeals court on Thursday removed Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis from the Georgia election interference case against Donald Trump and others but did not dismiss the indictment, leaving the future of the prosecution uncertain.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amazon workers strike at multiple facilities as Teamsters seek labor contract
item.description: Workers at seven Amazon facilities went on strike Thursday, an effort by the Teamsters to pressure the e-commerce company for a labor agreement during a key shopping period.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspect in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO will return to New York to face murder charges
item.description: The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO will return to New York to face murder charges after agreeing to be extradited Thursday during a court appearance in Pennsylvania where he was arrested last week after five days on the run.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, KRON4

item.title: Madison, Wisconsin, school shooting suspect was in contact with man accused of plotting his own attack: report
item.description: The teen shooter at a private Christian school in Madison, Wisconsin, was in contact with a man in California whom authorities say was plotting a mass shooting with her, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Union Pacific train derails after hitting tractor-trailer on Texas railroad tracks, killing two: video
item.description: Two Union Pacific employees are dead and three people are injured after a train hit a tractor-trailer stopped on the railroad tracks in Pecos, Texas, causing it to derail.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder: Death penalty on the table for suspect Luigi Mangione who faces federal charges
item.description: United Healthcare CEO murder suspect Luigi Mangione appeared in court Thursday and waived extradition, paving way to return to New York.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former top aide to New York City's mayor expected in court on criminal charges, AP sources say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams is expected in court Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to an administration beset by searches, resignations and the mayor's own indictment.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 184-foot floating Christmas tree collapses, killing 21-year-old man
item.description: A 21-year-old man was killed when a 184-foot floating Christmas tree collapsed in Brazil earlier this week. The incident occurred on Monday in the city of Maricá, which is located roughly 25 miles east of Rio de Janeiro.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: First severe bird flu case confirmed in US: CDC
item.description: Human cases of bird flu have been reported in the U.S. before, but infections were mild in those patients.
item.dc:creator: Trinity Velazquez, WGN 9

item.title: I tried the robot massage and here's how it compares
item.description: Massages aren't for everyone. But once you get one, you realize the benefits - it's relaxing and probably good for you in various ways. Now, one startup named Aescape is developing a robot to bring high-quality massages to the masses.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: 1 dead, 9 injured in Maryland shooting and fiery car crash: police
item.description: One person has died and nine others were injured following a shooting and related fiery car crash in Maryland on Tuesday, police said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, WGN 9
